sources.title: Pod Lifecycle
sources.scope_notes: Backs pod phases, container states, restart behavior, CrashLoopBackOff semantics, init-container sequencing, readiness-related lifecycle concepts, and general pod-state troubleshooting vocabulary.

sources.title: Debug Running Pods
sources.scope_notes: Backs use of describe, logs, events, exec, and YAML inspection to diagnose pod startup, scheduling, and runtime failures.

sources.title: Debug Services
sources.scope_notes: Backs service-level checks such as verifying Service existence, selector matching, EndpointSlice population, and the classic 'service has no endpoints' troubleshooting flow.

sources.title: Node Status
sources.scope_notes: Backs node conditions, Ready/NotReady/Unknown meanings, heartbeats, Lease objects, and node-level status fields exposed through the API.

sources.title: Node-pressure Eviction
sources.scope_notes: Backs kubelet eviction behavior under memory, disk, inode, and PID pressure, including eviction signals and threshold-driven remediation concepts.

sources.title: Troubleshooting Clusters
sources.scope_notes: Backs cluster-wide troubleshooting workflow, basic node and control-plane investigation, log locations, and example failure modes when nodes or control-plane components are down.

sources.title: Debugging Kubernetes nodes with crictl
sources.scope_notes: Backs direct CRI-level inspection of pods, containers, images, and logs on a node when kubelet/API-level access is insufficient or the API server is unavailable.

sources.title: kubeadm Implementation Details
sources.scope_notes: Backs kubeadm-managed control-plane layout, especially static pod manifest paths under /etc/kubernetes/manifests, host networking defaults, and local etcd static-pod behavior.

sources.title: Certificate Management with kubeadm
sources.scope_notes: Backs kubeadm certificate defaults, certificate validity periods, expiry inspection, and renewal workflows relevant to control-plane certificate-expiration incidents.

sources.title: Operating etcd clusters for Kubernetes
sources.scope_notes: Backs etcd’s role as the Kubernetes backing store, odd-member quorum guidance, backup and restore expectations, security considerations, and operational failure implications for the control plane.

sources.title: Services, Load Balancing, and Networking
sources.scope_notes: Backs the Kubernetes network model: pod IPs, pod-to-pod reachability expectations, Service abstractions, EndpointSlice involvement, and high-level networking architecture used in troubleshooting workflows.

sources.title: Service
sources.scope_notes: Backs Service types and behavior: ClusterIP, NodePort default range, LoadBalancer semantics, ExternalName, headless Services, selectors, DNS-based discovery, and readiness/endpoints relationships.

sources.title: Virtual IPs and Service Proxies
sources.scope_notes: Backs claims that Service IPs are virtual, kube-proxy programs packet-handling rules, and documents kube-proxy backend modes including IPVS deprecation and nftables/iptables behavior.

sources.title: DNS for Services and Pods
sources.scope_notes: Backs cluster DNS behavior, service and pod DNS records, namespace-qualified lookups, headless-service DNS results, and the role of cluster DNS in service discovery troubleshooting.

sources.title: Network Policies
sources.scope_notes: Backs NetworkPolicy semantics, ingress/egress controls, additive policy behavior, pod/namespace/IPBlock selectors, and the requirement for a network plugin that enforces NetworkPolicy.

sources.title: Logging Architecture
sources.scope_notes: Backs stdout/stderr logging expectations, node log-file locations, kubelet-managed log handling/rotation context, and sidecar-based logging patterns for file-writing applications.

sources.title: kubectl logs
sources.scope_notes: Backs exact kubectl logs behavior and flags such as container selection, follow mode, timestamps, tail, since, previous logs, and all-containers retrieval.

sources.title: Resource metrics pipeline
sources.scope_notes: Backs how kubectl top depends on Metrics API / metrics-server, how resource metrics flow from kubelet through metrics-server to the API server, and the scope of CPU/memory metrics available for pods and nodes.
